title: medicago rugosa f. incisa
intro: Medicago rugosa f. incisa, also known as the hairy medick or hop clover, is a perennial legume plant belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, primarily due to the presence of isoflavones and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Medicago rugosa f. incisa include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing potential anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusement, with the dried flowers and leaves being commonly used in traditional medicine.
